title: Refactor tests when checking for error exit codes
body:
Rather than checking for non-zero, we need to check for >0 to
distinguish between timeouts and error exit codes.

Signed-off-by: Jhon Honce <jhonce@redhat.com>

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *cevich*, *jwhonce* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Flibpod). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/master/OWNERS)~~ [jwhonce] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
Looks good, but I feel compelled to repeat my broken-record suggestion that tests should check for an explicit, well-defined status code (1, 126, 127), not just "some error"...
@edsantiago Agreed.  The new matcher allows that.  I just didn't have cycles to research those details.
@jwhonce my apologies - I didn't mean to imply that you should fix that in this PR. I just want to maintain awareness of the issue. Thanks!
:umbrella: The latest upstream changes (presumably #4181) made this pull request unmergeable. Please resolve the merge conflicts.
